name: Butterhorns
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften the yeast in warm water with 1 tablespoon (tbsp) of sugar. Set aside.
2. In a large bowl, combine scalded milk, shortening, sugar, and salt. Cool to lukewarm, then add the yeast mixture and mix well.
3. Add eggs. Measure flour one cup at a time, mixing well until the dough is smooth, soft, and elastic, and no longer sticky.
4. Knead lightly on a floured surface.
5. Form into a ball. Place in a warm bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm, draft-free place until doubled; about 2 to 2-1/2 hours with regular yeast.
6. Punch down the dough, knead lightly, and divide into thirds.
7. Roll each third into a 12-inch circle. Brush with melted butter.
8. Cut into 12 pie-shaped wedges and roll (starting at the wide end and ending at the point) each wedge into a crescent.
9. Brush each crescent with melted butter. Place on a greased baking sheet and let rise until very light (about an hour).
10. Bake at about 375°F for about 12 minutes or until light golden brown. Experiment with your oven, as the temperature can vary between 350°F and 400°F. If the rolls burn on the bottom before turning light brown, the oven is too hot.
